title: Relational Operators ⚖️
description: Compare values to ask true/false questions.

slides.title: Asking Questions ❓
slides.content:
In the last lesson, we passed `true` or `false` directly into the `if ()` statement.

But normally, we don't know the answer. We need to **evaluate** something to see if it is true or false. 

We do this by using **Relational Operators**.

slides.title: The Big Six ⚖️
slides.content:
There are 6 symbols you can use to compare two values:

- `==` (Equal To)
- `!=` (NOT Equal To)
- `<`  (Less Than)
- `>`  (Greater Than)
- `<=` (Less Than or Equal)
- `>=` (Greater Than or Equal)

quizData.question: Which operator checks if two things are EXACTLY the same?
quizData.explanation: In Java, a single '=' means 'assign this value'. A double '==' means 'compare these two to see if they are equal'.

slides.title: Combining with If 🤝
slides.content: Let's put our relational operators inside the `if` statement's parentheses.

quizData.question: If `int enemies = 0;`, will `if (enemies == 0) { System.out.println("Win"); }` print 'Win'?
quizData.explanation: 0 == 0 evaluates to `true`. Thus, the block runs.

slides.title: Not Equals (!=) 🚫
